pages.text: $(bold)Cached Production$() = Running factories using math instead of actually loading chunks

pages.title: The Problem
pages.text: In vanilla Minecraft: $(li)Factories require chunk loading (chunks must be active) $(li)Every machine ticks every frame $(li)Entities move, hoppers check neighbors, etc. $(li)$(bold)Result$(): Significant TPS (ticks per second) impact

pages.title: The FPSCompress Solution
pages.text: With cached production: $(li)$(bold)SIMULATING phase$(): Measure actual factory production rates (chunks loaded) $(li)$(bold)CACHED phase$(): Simulate production using fractional math (chunks $(bold)unloaded$()) $(li)$(bold)Result$(): Factory "runs" without loading CM dimension chunks$(br2)$(bold)Performance gain$(): 10-20 TPS improvement per complex factory (varies by factory)
